1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}

]}

[event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


oc



Suite: oc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by ResourceAddress (e.g. /cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15) instead of by DataType ("notification"), so ptp.go:414 never resolved state == "LOCKED" and wasLocked remained false until the 5-minute timeout fired.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized (affects: [It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)
• Evidence:

```

[FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395

```

```

DEBUG [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]

DEBUG [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

```

```

INFO [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms

INFO [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex

```

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME) as the key rather than the DataType string ("notification"). Consequently, at ptp.go:414, values["notification"].(string) returns "" (key not found), so the guard if !wasLocked && state == "LOCKED" at ptp.go:425 is never satisfied. After the full 5-minute soak window the tcEndChan fires and Fail("OS Clock was never LOCKED") is called at ptp.go:393. The clockOffset=0.000000 debug line (expected 15 ns from the event metric) independently confirms the same key-mismatch — values["metric"].(float64) also fails. The identical Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync: result: error failure appears in every parallel mode (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc) confirming the failure is systematic and code-level, not clock-hardware-specific. The runtime version of event.go (referenced at line 730 in CI, vs. 663 lines in the current local clone) indicates the file was modified between the last local commit (2026-07-08) and the CI run (2026-07-12); that change likely altered createStoredEvent to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) instead of v.DataType as the StoredEventValues key, while ptp.go:414 was not updated to match.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the breaking change was committed to main between 2026-07-08 and 2026-07-12 and is not in the local clone. Candidate: a commit modifying test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent that changed values[string(v.DataType)] = v.Value to values[v.Resource] = v.Value. Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate release-4.22 once the SHA is identified.

• Is it a flake? No — the failure is deterministic and reproduces across all parallel PTP modes in the same run; it is a code-level key mismatch, not a timing or environment transient.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent, ensure StoredEventValues is populated with string(v.DataType) (i.e., "notification" / "metric") as the map key, matching what test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–415 expects. Alternatively, update ptp.go:414–415 to look up by the correct key used in the stored event. The v1 and v2 paths in createStoredEvent must be consistent.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH




TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.

Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}

]}

[event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


oc



Suite: oc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by ResourceAddress (e.g. /cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15) instead of by DataType ("notification"), so ptp.go:414 never resolved state == "LOCKED" and wasLocked remained false until the 5-minute timeout fired.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized (affects: [It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)
• Evidence:

```

[FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395

```

```

DEBUG [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]

DEBUG [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

```

```

INFO [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms

INFO [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex

```

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME) as the key rather than the DataType string ("notification"). Consequently, at ptp.go:414, values["notification"].(string) returns "" (key not found), so the guard if !wasLocked && state == "LOCKED" at ptp.go:425 is never satisfied. After the full 5-minute soak window the tcEndChan fires and Fail("OS Clock was never LOCKED") is called at ptp.go:393. The clockOffset=0.000000 debug line (expected 15 ns from the event metric) independently confirms the same key-mismatch — values["metric"].(float64) also fails. The identical Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync: result: error failure appears in every parallel mode (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc) confirming the failure is systematic and code-level, not clock-hardware-specific. The runtime version of event.go (referenced at line 730 in CI, vs. 663 lines in the current local clone) indicates the file was modified between the last local commit (2026-07-08) and the CI run (2026-07-12); that change likely altered createStoredEvent to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) instead of v.DataType as the StoredEventValues key, while ptp.go:414 was not updated to match.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the breaking change was committed to main between 2026-07-08 and 2026-07-12 and is not in the local clone. Candidate: a commit modifying test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent that changed values[string(v.DataType)] = v.Value to values[v.Resource] = v.Value. Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate release-4.22 once the SHA is identified.

• Is it a flake? No — the failure is deterministic and reproduces across all parallel PTP modes in the same run; it is a code-level key mismatch, not a timing or environment transient.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent, ensure StoredEventValues is populated with string(v.DataType) (i.e., "notification" / "metric") as the map key, matching what test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–415 expects. Alternatively, update ptp.go:414–415 to look up by the correct key used in the stored event. The v1 and v2 paths in createStoredEvent must be consistent.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH




TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the breaking change was committed to main between 2026-07-08 and 2026-07-12 and is not in the local clone. Candidate: a commit modifying test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent that changed values[string(v.DataType)] = v.Value to values[v.Resource] = v.Value. Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate release-4.22 once the SHA is identified.

• Is it a flake? No — the failure is deterministic and reproduces across all parallel PTP modes in the same run; it is a code-level key mismatch, not a timing or environment transient.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent, ensure StoredEventValues is populated with string(v.DataType) (i.e., "notification" / "metric") as the map key, matching what test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–415 expects. Alternatively, update ptp.go:414–415 to look up by the correct key used in the stored event. The v1 and v2 paths in createStoredEvent must be consistent.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH




TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}

]}

[event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


oc



Suite: oc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by ResourceAddress (e.g. /cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15) instead of by DataType ("notification"), so ptp.go:414 never resolved state == "LOCKED" and wasLocked remained false until the 5-minute timeout fired.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized (affects: [It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)
• Evidence:

```

[FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395

```

```

DEBUG [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]

DEBUG [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

```

```

INFO [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms

INFO [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex

```

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME) as the key rather than the DataType string ("notification"). Consequently, at ptp.go:414, values["notification"].(string) returns "" (key not found), so the guard if !wasLocked && state == "LOCKED" at ptp.go:425 is never satisfied. After the full 5-minute soak window the tcEndChan fires and Fail("OS Clock was never LOCKED") is called at ptp.go:393. The clockOffset=0.000000 debug line (expected 15 ns from the event metric) independently confirms the same key-mismatch — values["metric"].(float64) also fails. The identical Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync: result: error failure appears in every parallel mode (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc) confirming the failure is systematic and code-level, not clock-hardware-specific. The runtime version of event.go (referenced at line 730 in CI, vs. 663 lines in the current local clone) indicates the file was modified between the last local commit (2026-07-08) and the CI run (2026-07-12); that change likely altered createStoredEvent to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) instead of v.DataType as the StoredEventValues key, while ptp.go:414 was not updated to match.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the breaking change was committed to main between 2026-07-08 and 2026-07-12 and is not in the local clone. Candidate: a commit modifying test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent that changed values[string(v.DataType)] = v.Value to values[v.Resource] = v.Value. Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate release-4.22 once the SHA is identified.

• Is it a flake? No — the failure is deterministic and reproduces across all parallel PTP modes in the same run; it is a code-level key mismatch, not a timing or environment transient.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go:createStoredEvent, ensure StoredEventValues is populated with string(v.DataType) (i.e., "notification" / "metric") as the map key, matching what test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–415 expects. Alternatively, update ptp.go:414–415 to look up by the correct key used in the stored event. The v1 and v2 paths in createStoredEvent must be consistent.

No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.

1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.
