A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 never reaches LOCKED state, cascading across all sync-dependent suites; T-BC test code requires lab hardware that doesn't exist.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~33/38 failures — GM in FREERUN (ts2phc FREERUN while DPLL LOCKED), cascading to all BC/slave sync tests (tgm: 11, dualfollower: 4, dualnicbc + dualnicbcha: ~18 implied)

With cnfdf32's ts2phc stuck in FREERUN, clock class never reaches 6, ptp4l never propagates a valid grandmaster signal downstream, and every test in every suite that waits for slave LOCKED state (ptp4l, phc2sys, cloud-event-proxy metrics) times out. The ens2f0np0 interface on cnfdf31 emitting no openshift_ptp_clock_state metric at all is a downstream symptom of the same GM-side failure.


• 1/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid hardware solution (all suite, SynchronizedBeforeSuite)

PR #205 (84723c8) merged a new deterministic six-constraint T-BC solver to main. The rdu2.redhat.com CI lab does not expose a matching WPC NIC topology (receiver + 2 same-NIC transmitters + local WPC GM). Solver retries 3× and always returns empty, aborting the entire TBC parallel suite before any test runs.


• ~4/38 failures — Possible secondary: ptp4l PMC protocol regression (bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET seen in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha logs)

May overlap with the GM-FREERUN group if the release-5.0 image has a ptp4l build regression affecting PMC command parsing, but insufficient evidence to split further without dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suite analyses.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfolloweralltgmdualnicbcdualnicbcha
GM FREERUN / no LOCKED✓ (4)—✓ (11)✓ (~implied)✓ (~implied)
T-BC solver / no hardware—✓ (1)———
PMC bad commandpartial——✓✓

The GM FREERUN pattern is cross-suite and accounts for the overwhelming majority of failures. The T-BC solver failure is entirely suite-specific (all/TBC parallel) and is a code regression, not an infra issue.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate cnfdf32 ts2phc immediately (highest leverage — fixes ~33 failures). Check whether the release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon image has a regression in ts2phc startup, holdover recovery, or GNSS lock acquisition. Specifically: compare ts2phc behavior between the last passing nightly and this run; check if the ts2phc termination tests earlier in the serial run leave ts2phc in an unrecoverable state (consider adding a ts2phc process health check/reset in AfterEach).

2. Gate the TBC parallel suite behind a hardware capability check (fixes 1 SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort + unblocks the suite). Add an env-var guard (e.g., ENABLE_TBC_TESTS) or a pre-suite hardware probe before enabling PR #205's T-BC code path in nightly CI. Alternatively, provision T-BC-capable hardware in rdu2.redhat.com and verify the L2 discovery result satisfies all six solver constraints.

3. Audit PMC command handling in the release-5.0 image. The bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET errors in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha indicate a possible ptp4l build or config regression that could independently block sync negotiation even if ts2phc recovers. Cross-reference with the release-5.0 changelog since the last passing nightly.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (dominant cause is a release-5.0 image regression in ts2phc state management; secondary is a newly merged test code path incompatible with CI lab hardware)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 33+ of 38 failures share a single infra/image root cause; the PTP grandmaster is non-functional, making the entire CI job unreliable as a signal for the release-5.0 branch.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because node cnfdf31 (the dual-follower slave) never achieves PTP LOCKED state — both follower interfaces (ens2f0np0, ens2f3np3) fail to lock to the upstream grandmaster within the 600s window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Dual-follower slave node never reached LOCKED state (affects: all 4 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority", "PTP Slave Clock Sync [parallel]")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri[cs...]

```

```

[dualfollower-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync:

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

From ptp4l_parser.go:300-305, the LISTENING role with ClockStateFreeRun means ptp4l is running but has not received valid ANNOUNCE messages from any master — the node is waiting for a grandmaster it cannot see. The ens2f0np0 not found in openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics means the daemon published zero clock-state metrics for that interface; ptp4l either never emitted state-change log lines for it, or the metric was never initialised (daemon-side, in log_parsing.go / metrics.go in linuxptp-daemon).


Both symptoms are consistent with the same underlying failure: cnfdf31's dual-follower ptp4l instance never received a PTP ANNOUNCE from the grandmaster on either upstream port, causing it to remain in LISTENING/FREERUN throughout the entire 600s timeout window.


• Root Cause: The dual-follower slave node cnfdf31 could not lock to the PTP grandmaster. ens2f3np3 sat in LISTENING state (ptp4l running, no master discovered), and ens2f0np0 produced no openshift_ptp_clock_state metric at all — indicating the daemon never processed any state-change event for that interface. This is consistent with a network path or grandmaster availability problem at test start, or with a ptp4l-level regression preventing slave negotiation. Notably, other suites in the same CI run show a separate but related symptom: bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from PMC across dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, suggesting possible ptp4l protocol-level issues in the release-5.0 image under test.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown. The backport-check script (scripts/check_backport.py) was not present in the workspace. The release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon image is built from the openshift/ptp-operator release-5.0 branch (confirmed: PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-5.0, USE_UPSTREAM=false → build exits before building upstream daemon). Recent commits to release-5.0 of openshift/ptp-operator or its vendored linuxptp binary should be investigated.

• Is it a flake? Possibly — the clean LISTENING state (not a crash, just no master seen) is consistent with a transient network/grandmaster availability issue. However, the suite-wide failure pattern across dualnicbc/dualnicbcha (PMC bad command errors) and tgm (clock class never reaches 6) in the same job run suggests a systemic problem in the release-5.0 production image rather than a one-node flake.

• Suggested Fix: First retry the job to distinguish infrastructure flake from code regression. If the PMC bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET errors in other suites reproduce, audit recent commits to the release-5.0 branch of openshift/ptp-operator and its vendored ptp4l/pmc binary for protocol-compatibility changes; in particular, verify that pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET still works correctly against the ptp4l version in the built image.

Suite Issue Class: test_failure (slave node



TL;DR: WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 never reaches LOCKED state, cascading across all sync-dependent suites; T-BC test code requires lab hardware that doesn't exist.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~33/38 failures — GM in FREERUN (ts2phc FREERUN while DPLL LOCKED), cascading to all BC/slave sync tests (tgm: 11, dualfollower: 4, dualnicbc + dualnicbcha: ~18 implied)

With cnfdf32's ts2phc stuck in FREERUN, clock class never reaches 6, ptp4l never propagates a valid grandmaster signal downstream, and every test in every suite that waits for slave LOCKED state (ptp4l, phc2sys, cloud-event-proxy metrics) times out. The ens2f0np0 interface on cnfdf31 emitting no openshift_ptp_clock_state metric at all is a downstream symptom of the same GM-side failure.


• 1/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid hardware solution (all suite, SynchronizedBeforeSuite)

PR #205 (84723c8) merged a new deterministic six-constraint T-BC solver to main. The rdu2.redhat.com CI lab does not expose a matching WPC NIC topology (receiver + 2 same-NIC transmitters + local WPC GM). Solver retries 3× and always returns empty, aborting the entire TBC parallel suite before any test runs.


• ~4/38 failures — Possible secondary: ptp4l PMC protocol regression (bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET seen in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha logs)

May overlap with the GM-FREERUN group if the release-5.0 image has a ptp4l build regression affecting PMC command parsing, but insufficient evidence to split further without dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suite analyses.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfolloweralltgmdualnicbcdualnicbcha
GM FREERUN / no LOCKED✓ (4)—✓ (11)✓ (~implied)✓ (~implied)
T-BC solver / no hardware—✓ (1)———
PMC bad commandpartial——✓✓

The GM FREERUN pattern is cross-suite and accounts for the overwhelming majority of failures. The T-BC solver failure is entirely suite-specific (all/TBC parallel) and is a code regression, not an infra issue.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate cnfdf32 ts2phc immediately (highest leverage — fixes ~33 failures). Check whether the release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon image has a regression in ts2phc startup, holdover recovery, or GNSS lock acquisition. Specifically: compare ts2phc behavior between the last passing nightly and this run; check if the ts2phc termination tests earlier in the serial run leave ts2phc in an unrecoverable state (consider adding a ts2phc process health check/reset in AfterEach).

2. Gate the TBC parallel suite behind a hardware capability check (fixes 1 SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort + unblocks the suite). Add an env-var guard (e.g., ENABLE_TBC_TESTS) or a pre-suite hardware probe before enabling PR #205's T-BC code path in nightly CI. Alternatively, provision T-BC-capable hardware in rdu2.redhat.com and verify the L2 discovery result satisfies all six solver constraints.

3. Audit PMC command handling in the release-5.0 image. The bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET errors in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha indicate a possible ptp4l build or config regression that could independently block sync negotiation even if ts2phc recovers. Cross-reference with the release-5.0 changelog since the last passing nightly.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (dominant cause is a release-5.0 image regression in ts2phc state management; secondary is a newly merged test code path incompatible with CI lab hardware)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 33+ of 38 failures share a single infra/image root cause; the PTP grandmaster is non-functional, making the entire CI job unreliable as a signal for the release-5.0 branch.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because node cnfdf31 (the dual-follower slave) never achieves PTP LOCKED state — both follower interfaces (ens2f0np0, ens2f3np3) fail to lock to the upstream grandmaster within the 600s window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Dual-follower slave node never reached LOCKED state (affects: all 4 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority", "PTP Slave Clock Sync [parallel]")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri[cs...]

```

```

[dualfollower-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync:

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

From ptp4l_parser.go:300-305, the LISTENING role with ClockStateFreeRun means ptp4l is running but has not received valid ANNOUNCE messages from any master — the node is waiting for a grandmaster it cannot see. The ens2f0np0 not found in openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics means the daemon published zero clock-state metrics for that interface; ptp4l either never emitted state-change log lines for it, or the metric was never initialised (daemon-side, in log_parsing.go / metrics.go in linuxptp-daemon).


Both symptoms are consistent with the same underlying failure: cnfdf31's dual-follower ptp4l instance never received a PTP ANNOUNCE from the grandmaster on either upstream port, causing it to remain in LISTENING/FREERUN throughout the entire 600s timeout window.


• Root Cause: The dual-follower slave node cnfdf31 could not lock to the PTP grandmaster. ens2f3np3 sat in LISTENING state (ptp4l running, no master discovered), and ens2f0np0 produced no openshift_ptp_clock_state metric at all — indicating the daemon never processed any state-change event for that interface. This is consistent with a network path or grandmaster availability problem at test start, or with a ptp4l-level regression preventing slave negotiation. Notably, other suites in the same CI run show a separate but related symptom: bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from PMC across dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, suggesting possible ptp4l protocol-level issues in the release-5.0 image under test.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown. The backport-check script (scripts/check_backport.py) was not present in the workspace. The release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon image is built from the openshift/ptp-operator release-5.0 branch (confirmed: PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-5.0, USE_UPSTREAM=false → build exits before building upstream daemon). Recent commits to release-5.0 of openshift/ptp-operator or its vendored linuxptp binary should be investigated.

• Is it a flake? Possibly — the clean LISTENING state (not a crash, just no master seen) is consistent with a transient network/grandmaster availability issue. However, the suite-wide failure pattern across dualnicbc/dualnicbcha (PMC bad command errors) and tgm (clock class never reaches 6) in the same job run suggests a systemic problem in the release-5.0 production image rather than a one-node flake.

• Suggested Fix: First retry the job to distinguish infrastructure flake from code regression. If the PMC bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET errors in other suites reproduce, audit recent commits to the release-5.0 branch of openshift/ptp-operator and its vendored ptp4l/pmc binary for protocol-compatibility changes; in particular, verify that pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET still works correctly against the ptp4l version in the built image.

Suite Issue Class: test_failure (slave node



TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 32 | Suites: dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm



TL;DR: All 32 failures trace to a single unlocked T-GM (ts2phc FREERUN) and a broken pmc command interface cascading across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — T-GM ts2phc stuck in FREERUN (clockClass 248) while dpll=LOCKED (tgm: 10, dualnicbc: ~5, dualnicbcha: ~4): The GM node cnfdf32 never emits clockClass 6 because ts2phc_parser.go accumulates "source ts not valid" hits and forces ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN, overriding a confirmed dpll LOCK. This starves every downstream suite — BC slave ports on the dualnic nodes have no locked upstream grandmaster to sync to, so they remain MASTER/FREERUN themselves, cascading into all WPC GM state, holdover, recovery, and event-timeout failures across all three suites.

• ~13/32 failures — pmc rejects GET PARENT_DATA_SET as two "bad command" tokens (dualnicbc: 5, dualnicbcha: 4, cross-suite overlap ~4): Tests calling ptptesthelper.go:106 via pmc interactive mode receive bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET for every query, blocking all clock-identity discovery. The two-token error pattern is diagnostic of either (a) the unpinned linuxptp RPM in Dockerfile.ocp updating to ≥4.2 where management_enabled=0 is the new default, or (b) a code path in pmc.go passing "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single unsplit argument to exec.Command. This blocks "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "higher priority" tests in both dualnic suites.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
T-GM FREERUN (ts2phc=0, dpll=1)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (primary — all WPC tests)
pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SETYesYesNo

• Shared across all suites: The absent/unlocked grandmaster. The tgm suite owns the root of this failure; dualnic suites are casualties of it.
• Shared across dualnicbc + dualnicbcha only: The pmc command-format breakage. Identical error strings, identical affected test names — this is one systemic defect, not two independent bugs.
• Suite-specific: No failures appear to be genuinely isolated to a single suite; all are manifestations of the two root causes above.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the T-GM ts2phc FREERUN (highest leverage — unblocks ~19 failures across all suites): On node cnfdf32, determine whether a recent linuxptp update changed the ts2phc log format so that regularTS2PhcRegex in ts2phc_parser.go:14–23 no longer matches, causing silent NoSourceTSCount accumulation. If the regex is the cause, update it to match the new format. Also verify whether commit fd6ff1e (upstream-sync, merged 2026-06-30 on main) was cherry-picked to release-5.0 and introduced a regression in the ts2phc state machine.

2. Fix the pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET command breakage (unblocks ~13 failures in dualnic suites): Pin the linuxptp RPM to a known-working version in linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp (currently yum install linuxptp with no version). As a parallel fix, verify that pmc.go and ptptesthelper.go:106 pass GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate arguments to exec.Command rather than as a single space-delimited string. Additionally, explicitly set management_enabled 1 in the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go to prevent linuxptp ≥4.2 from silently disabling management queries.

3. Investigate whether fd6ff1e needs a backport or revert on release-5.0: Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate fd6ff1e release-5.0 (once available) to confirm whether the 2026-06-30 upstream-sync commit is present in the 5.0 image. If it introduced the ts2phc parser regression or the pmc argument bug, revert or cherry-pick the targeted fix to release-5.0 rather than carrying the full sync.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Deterministic, reproducible across suites in a single run; not a flake, not an infra timeout — the metrics and log evidence point to a code/package change breaking both the GM state machine and the pmc interface.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The T-GM never locks, meaning the entire PTP sync hierarchy is non-functional; 32/32 failures, all three suites affected, no tests pass in the sync-dependent path.)




Per-Suite Details



dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Every test in the dualnicbc suite fails because the Boundary Clock (BC) slave port ens2f0np0 never achieves SLAVE state (stuck at clockClass 248 / FREERUN), which cascades into pmc query failures, stale metrics, event timeouts, and outage-recovery timeouts; the T-GM's own ts2phc is also in FREERUN, indicating the root failure is the absence of a locked upstream grandmaster.



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: pmc returns bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

Identical errors appear for ptp4l.0.config in the "higher priority" test, and identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same node.

• Root Cause: ptptesthelper.go:106 spawns pmc -u -b 0 -f /var/run/ptp4l.N.config in interactive mode via goexpect and sends GET PARENT_DATA_SET\n (see linuxptp-daemon/pkg/pmc/pmc.go:17,49,147). The bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET output is pmc's own parser rejecting each token as an unrecognized command. This is consistent with a linuxptp version in the release-5.0 image where the pmc interactive-mode command table does not include GET / SET prefixes, or with management_enabled=0 on ptp4l (added in linuxptp ≥4.2 as the default) causing ptp4l to respond with NOT\_SUPPORTED which pmc renders as "bad command". The Dockerfile.ocp (linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp:12) installs linuxptp from RHEL9 repos with no version pin, so an unintended package update to ≥4.2 is a plausible trigger.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown – no pinned linuxptp version; the upstream-sync PR fd6ff1e ("CNF-22056/CNF-22693/CNF-25167/OCPBUGS-85586/OCPBUGS-93744", merged 2026-06-30 on main of linuxptp-daemon) is only on main and would not affect release-5.0 images unless cherry-picked. Verify with: python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate fd6ff1e release-5.0
• Is it a flake? No – same error on both ptp4l.0.config and ptp4l.1.config, and reproduced identically in dualnicbcha on the same run.
• Suggested Fix: Pin linuxptp to a specific RPM version in Dockerfile.ocp, or explicitly add management_enabled 1 to the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go so management queries work regardless of linuxptp version default.

• Group 2: BC slave port stuck as MASTER, clockClass 248 (FREERUN) (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source", "PTP Outage recovery: interface taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

: 248 to equal : 6

incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

: false to be true

```

From test discovery output:

```

INFO [testconfig.go: 2172] ptptConfig: test-bc-master1, master



TL;DR: WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 never reaches LOCKED state, cascading across all sync-dependent suites; T-BC test code requires lab hardware that doesn't exist.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~33/38 failures — GM in FREERUN (ts2phc FREERUN while DPLL LOCKED), cascading to all BC/slave sync tests (tgm: 11, dualfollower: 4, dualnicbc + dualnicbcha: ~18 implied)

With cnfdf32's ts2phc stuck in FREERUN, clock class never reaches 6, ptp4l never propagates a valid grandmaster signal downstream, and every test in every suite that waits for slave LOCKED state (ptp4l, phc2sys, cloud-event-proxy metrics) times out. The ens2f0np0 interface on cnfdf31 emitting no openshift_ptp_clock_state metric at all is a downstream symptom of the same GM-side failure.


• 1/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid hardware solution (all suite, SynchronizedBeforeSuite)

PR #205 (84723c8) merged a new deterministic six-constraint T-BC solver to main. The rdu2.redhat.com CI lab does not expose a matching WPC NIC topology (receiver + 2 same-NIC transmitters + local WPC GM). Solver retries 3× and always returns empty, aborting the entire TBC parallel suite before any test runs.


• ~4/38 failures — Possible secondary: ptp4l PMC protocol regression (bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET seen in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha logs)

May overlap with the GM-FREERUN group if the release-5.0 image has a ptp4l build regression affecting PMC command parsing, but insufficient evidence to split further without dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suite analyses.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfolloweralltgmdualnicbcdualnicbcha
GM FREERUN / no LOCKED✓ (4)—✓ (11)✓ (~implied)✓ (~implied)
T-BC solver / no hardware—✓ (1)———
PMC bad commandpartial——✓✓

The GM FREERUN pattern is cross-suite and accounts for the overwhelming majority of failures. The T-BC solver failure is entirely suite-specific (all/TBC parallel) and is a code regression, not an infra issue.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate cnfdf32 ts2phc immediately (highest leverage — fixes ~33 failures). Check whether the release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon image has a regression in ts2phc startup, holdover recovery, or GNSS lock acquisition. Specifically: compare ts2phc behavior between the last passing nightly and this run; check if the ts2phc termination tests earlier in the serial run leave ts2phc in an unrecoverable state (consider adding a ts2phc process health check/reset in AfterEach).

2. Gate the TBC parallel suite behind a hardware capability check (fixes 1 SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort + unblocks the suite). Add an env-var guard (e.g., ENABLE_TBC_TESTS) or a pre-suite hardware probe before enabling PR #205's T-BC code path in nightly CI. Alternatively, provision T-BC-capable hardware in rdu2.redhat.com and verify the L2 discovery result satisfies all six solver constraints.

3. Audit PMC command handling in the release-5.0 image. The bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET errors in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha indicate a possible ptp4l build or config regression that could independently block sync negotiation even if ts2phc recovers. Cross-reference with the release-5.0 changelog since the last passing nightly.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (dominant cause is a release-5.0 image regression in ts2phc state management; secondary is a newly merged test code path incompatible with CI lab hardware)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 33+ of 38 failures share a single infra/image root cause; the PTP grandmaster is non-functional, making the entire CI job unreliable as a signal for the release-5.0 branch.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because node cnfdf31 (the dual-follower slave) never achieves PTP LOCKED state — both follower interfaces (ens2f0np0, ens2f3np3) fail to lock to the upstream grandmaster within the 600s window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Dual-follower slave node never reached LOCKED state (affects: all 4 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority", "PTP Slave Clock Sync [parallel]")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri[cs...]

```

```

[dualfollower-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync:

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

From ptp4l_parser.go:300-305, the LISTENING role with ClockStateFreeRun means ptp4l is running but has not received valid ANNOUNCE messages from any master — the node is waiting for a grandmaster it cannot see. The ens2f0np0 not found in openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics means the daemon published zero clock-state metrics for that interface; ptp4l either never emitted state-change log lines for it, or the metric was never initialised (daemon-side, in log_parsing.go / metrics.go in linuxptp-daemon).


Both symptoms are consistent with the same underlying failure: cnfdf31's dual-follower ptp4l instance never received a PTP ANNOUNCE from the grandmaster on either upstream port, causing it to remain in LISTENING/FREERUN throughout the entire 600s timeout window.


• Root Cause: The dual-follower slave node cnfdf31 could not lock to the PTP grandmaster. ens2f3np3 sat in LISTENING state (ptp4l running, no master discovered), and ens2f0np0 produced no openshift_ptp_clock_state metric at all — indicating the daemon never processed any state-change event for that interface. This is consistent with a network path or grandmaster availability problem at test start, or with a ptp4l-level regression preventing slave negotiation. Notably, other suites in the same CI run show a separate but related symptom: bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from PMC across dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, suggesting possible ptp4l protocol-level issues in the release-5.0 image under test.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown. The backport-check script (scripts/check_backport.py) was not present in the workspace. The release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon image is built from the openshift/ptp-operator release-5.0 branch (confirmed: PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-5.0, USE_UPSTREAM=false → build exits before building upstream daemon). Recent commits to release-5.0 of openshift/ptp-operator or its vendored linuxptp binary should be investigated.

• Is it a flake? Possibly — the clean LISTENING state (not a crash, just no master seen) is consistent with a transient network/grandmaster availability issue. However, the suite-wide failure pattern across dualnicbc/dualnicbcha (PMC bad command errors) and tgm (clock class never reaches 6) in the same job run suggests a systemic problem in the release-5.0 production image rather than a one-node flake.

• Suggested Fix: First retry the job to distinguish infrastructure flake from code regression. If the PMC bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET errors in other suites reproduce, audit recent commits to the release-5.0 branch of openshift/ptp-operator and its vendored ptp4l/pmc binary for protocol-compatibility changes; in particular, verify that pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET still works correctly against the ptp4l version in the built image.

Suite Issue Class: test_failure (slave node



TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.

The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).

Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), wiping 22 tests; 3 more failed from a metric alias bug.



Root Cause Groups


• 22/25 failures — T-GM stuck at clock class 248 (FREERUN), GNSS lock never achieved (suites: tgm 5, dualnicbc 9, dualnicbcha 8)

The WPC grandmaster (linuxptp-daemon-vzbr9) never transitioned to clock class 6 for the entire ~32-minute run. With no locked upstream source, every BC's upstream port (ens2f0np0) stayed MASTER (BMCA had nothing better to announce), pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET returned bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET on all retries, and every downstream sync/event/metric assertion failed. The prime suspect is commit 9ba6326 ("CNF-24996: Decouple GNSS and DPLL", merged to release-5.0 ~Jun 22) which explicitly removes GNSS state from DPLL state calculation in pkg/dpll/dpll.go and pkg/event/event.go — breaking the ts2phc → DPLL → ptp4l clock-class-6 promotion pathway for WPC hardware.


• 3/25 failures — Interface alias mismatch: daemon labels vs. test query (suite: dualfollower 3)

The release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon computes an alias via pkg/alias.GetAliasValue() (regex strips npN suffix + trailing digit), publishing openshift_ptp_clock_state as iface="ens2fx". The main-branch test helper (test/pkg/metrics/metrics.go:201–222, commit f28aceb1) only tries the raw name (ens2f0np0) and a naive last-char alias (ens2f0npx) — never ens2fx. Every metric lookup returns "not found" and each test exhausts its 600s Eventually window.



Cross-Suite Patterns


SuiteGM FREERUNAlias mismatch
tgmYES (direct — GM is the SUT)—
dualnicbcYES (cascaded from GM)—
dualnicbchaYES (cascaded from GM)—
dualfollowerContributing (no upstream sync)YES (primary label)

• tgm / dualnicbc / dualnicbcha share a single root cause: the upstream GM not locking. The failure signature is identical across all three (clockClass=248, role=MASTER, bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET).
• dualfollower is partially unique: its primary reported failure mode is the alias lookup bug (ens2fx not found), but the concurrent GM lock failure means the slaves had no valid source to sync to anyway — both issues compound.
• No suite-specific infra or build anomalies were identified beyond these two root causes.


Actionable Recommendations


1. Investigate/revert 9ba6326 on release-5.0 — Run check_backport.py 9ba6326 release-5.0 to confirm the commit is present, then assess whether re-coupling GNSS state into DPLL state calculation (or providing an alternative signal path) restores clock-class-6 promotion on WPC hardware. This single fix resolves 22/25 failures.

2. Fix the alias lookup in test/pkg/metrics/metrics.go — Replace the naive npx alias candidate with logic matching the daemon's GetAliasValue() regex (strip npN suffix + trailing port digit, e.g. ens2f0np0 → ens2fx). This resolves 3/25 failures independently of the GM issue.

3. Verify GNSS signal on the T-GM lab node — If 9ba6326 is not present on release-5.0, rule out a transient hardware/GPS-antenna issue by checking GNSS lock history for cnfdf31/linuxptp-daemon-vzbr9 across adjacent nightly runs. A single-run outage with clean recovery would indicate lab infra rather than a code regression.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

Overall Severity: CRITICAL




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower


Now I have enough to write the full analysis.



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests time out because the test's metric-lookup function cannot find openshift_ptp_clock_state for the slave interfaces — the release-5.0 daemon publishes the aliased iface label ens2fx, but the main-branch test code only queries ens2f0np0 and ens2f0npx, never ens2fx.



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Alias mismatch between daemon metric labels and test metric query (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")

• Evidence:

```

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com,

aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics: [...]

```

(from junit-failures.txt, identical for all 3 tests; "available op…" truncated in evidence files but the pattern is the same for both slave interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3)


```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3])

```

(from junit-failures.txt, each test timed out after 600 s)


Test scripts run from main, images from release-5.0:

```

+ export TEST_BRANCH=main

+ export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-5.0

+ export IMG_VERSION=release-5.0

```

(from ci-operator-build-log.txt lines 37–42)


• Root Cause: The release-5.0 linuxptp-daemon publishes openshift_ptp_clock_state using an alias computed by pkg/alias.GetAliasValue() (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/utils/utils.go, regex ^(.+?)(\d+)(?:np\d+)?(\..+)?$): for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 this strips the npN suffix and trailing port digit, producing ens2fx. The metric is therefore published as iface="ens2fx". The main-branch test helper (test/pkg/metrics/metrics.go:201–222, commit f28aceb1) only tries the raw name (ens2f0np0) and a naive last-character alias (ens2f0npx); it never tries ens2fx. Because ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 are ports on the same Intel NIC with a single PHC group, the daemon's calculateAliases does not fall back to original names (fallback only fires when two distinct PHC IDs share the same alias), so ens2fx is the only label emitted. The metric lookup always returns "not found", and each test exhausts its 600 s Eventually retry window.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Commit f28aceb1 ("Make getMetic to check for ifname and alias", merged via PR #193, March 11 2026) is the most recent change touching this logic. It partially improved the fallback but introduced an incorrect alias candidate (ens2f0npx instead of ens2fx). The underlying incompatibility (daemon aliasing vs. test aliasing) was latent before this PR; that PR did not cherry-pick to release-5.0. The daemon-side alias feature in linuxptp-daemon release-5.0 (pkg/alias/manager.go::calculateAliases, pkg/utils/utils.go::GetAliasValue) pre-dates this run and is present in the current release-5.0 build.

• Is it a flake? No — deterministic regression; the alias mismatch will reproduce on every run with this hardware and these branches.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/metrics/metrics.go::getMetric(), add GetAliasValue(aIf) (imported from linuxptp-daemon/pkg/utils or reimplemented with the same regex) as a third candidate in ifCandidates so the test uses the same aliasing logic as the daemon. The one-liner fix: ifCandidates = append(ifCandidates, utils.GetAliasValue(aIf)) after the existing ens2f0npx candidate. This must be done on main and then backported to any release branch that uses the same test runner.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (9 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 9 failures trace back to the T-GM (Grandmaster) never reaching clock class 6 (LOCKED) — clock class stays at 248 (FREE_RUN) across every node — so the BC has no valid master to sync from, causing every downstream sync, event, and metric assertion to fail.


Root Cause Groups:


• **Group 1: BC cannot sync — GM and BC both stuck at clock c


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

• Evidence:
