Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 32 | Suites: dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm



TL;DR: All 32 failures trace to a single unlocked T-GM (ts2phc FREERUN) and a broken pmc command interface cascading across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — T-GM ts2phc stuck in FREERUN (clockClass 248) while dpll=LOCKED (tgm: 10, dualnicbc: ~5, dualnicbcha: ~4): The GM node cnfdf32 never emits clockClass 6 because ts2phc_parser.go accumulates "source ts not valid" hits and forces ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN, overriding a confirmed dpll LOCK. This starves every downstream suite — BC slave ports on the dualnic nodes have no locked upstream grandmaster to sync to, so they remain MASTER/FREERUN themselves, cascading into all WPC GM state, holdover, recovery, and event-timeout failures across all three suites.

• ~13/32 failures — pmc rejects GET PARENT_DATA_SET as two "bad command" tokens (dualnicbc: 5, dualnicbcha: 4, cross-suite overlap ~4): Tests calling ptptesthelper.go:106 via pmc interactive mode receive bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET for every query, blocking all clock-identity discovery. The two-token error pattern is diagnostic of either (a) the unpinned linuxptp RPM in Dockerfile.ocp updating to ≥4.2 where management_enabled=0 is the new default, or (b) a code path in pmc.go passing "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single unsplit argument to exec.Command. This blocks "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "higher priority" tests in both dualnic suites.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
T-GM FREERUN (ts2phc=0, dpll=1)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (primary — all WPC tests)
pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SETYesYesNo

• Shared across all suites: The absent/unlocked grandmaster. The tgm suite owns the root of this failure; dualnic suites are casualties of it.
• Shared across dualnicbc + dualnicbcha only: The pmc command-format breakage. Identical error strings, identical affected test names — this is one systemic defect, not two independent bugs.
• Suite-specific: No failures appear to be genuinely isolated to a single suite; all are manifestations of the two root causes above.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the T-GM ts2phc FREERUN (highest leverage — unblocks ~19 failures across all suites): On node cnfdf32, determine whether a recent linuxptp update changed the ts2phc log format so that regularTS2PhcRegex in ts2phc_parser.go:14–23 no longer matches, causing silent NoSourceTSCount accumulation. If the regex is the cause, update it to match the new format. Also verify whether commit fd6ff1e (upstream-sync, merged 2026-06-30 on main) was cherry-picked to release-5.0 and introduced a regression in the ts2phc state machine.

2. Fix the pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET command breakage (unblocks ~13 failures in dualnic suites): Pin the linuxptp RPM to a known-working version in linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp (currently yum install linuxptp with no version). As a parallel fix, verify that pmc.go and ptptesthelper.go:106 pass GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate arguments to exec.Command rather than as a single space-delimited string. Additionally, explicitly set management_enabled 1 in the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go to prevent linuxptp ≥4.2 from silently disabling management queries.

3. Investigate whether fd6ff1e needs a backport or revert on release-5.0: Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate fd6ff1e release-5.0 (once available) to confirm whether the 2026-06-30 upstream-sync commit is present in the 5.0 image. If it introduced the ts2phc parser regression or the pmc argument bug, revert or cherry-pick the targeted fix to release-5.0 rather than carrying the full sync.

(Deterministic, reproducible across suites in a single run; not a flake, not an infra timeout — the metrics and log evidence point to a code/package change breaking both the GM state machine and the pmc interface.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 32 | Suites: dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm



TL;DR: All 32 failures trace to a single unlocked T-GM (ts2phc FREERUN) and a broken pmc command interface cascading across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — T-GM ts2phc stuck in FREERUN (clockClass 248) while dpll=LOCKED (tgm: 10, dualnicbc: ~5, dualnicbcha: ~4): The GM node cnfdf32 never emits clockClass 6 because ts2phc_parser.go accumulates "source ts not valid" hits and forces ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN, overriding a confirmed dpll LOCK. • ~13/32 failures — pmc rejects GET PARENT_DATA_SET as two "bad command" tokens (dualnicbc: 5, dualnicbcha: 4, cross-suite overlap ~4): Tests calling ptptesthelper.go:106 via pmc interactive mode receive bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET for every query, blocking all clock-identity discovery. 2. Fix the pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET command breakage (unblocks ~13 failures in dualnic suites): Pin the linuxptp RPM to a known-working version in linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp (currently yum install linuxptp with no version). As a parallel fix, verify that pmc.go and ptptesthelper.go:106 pass GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate arguments to exec.Command rather than as a single space-delimited string. Additionally, explicitly set management_enabled 1 in the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go to prevent linuxptp ≥4.2 from silently disabling management queries.

INFO [testconfig.go: 2172] ptptConfig: test-bc-master1, master



TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).

Cross-Suite CI Failure Summary


Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38 | Suites: dualnicbc, tbc, all



TL;DR: Two main-branch regressions — a broken pmc invocation and new T-BC hardware constraints — plus a cascading BC FREERUN condition account for all 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~33/38 failures — BC ptp4l stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), ens2f0np0 never transitioning to SLAVE: The dualnicbc suite's boundary clock does not lock to the grandmaster. This cascades into every downstream test that depends on a locked BC: sync verification, metric presence, clock-class event checks, recovery tests, and phc2sys restart tests. This is the dominant failure mode across the dualnicbc suite.

• 3/38 failures — pmc CLI invocation bug (commit 3b93c9f, Jun 9 2026): getClockIDViaPMC() in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go passes GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate positional CLI arguments. The pmc binary rejects both tokens as bad command, so clock-ID lookups always fail, causing Eventually blocks in sync tests to time out deterministically. Affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority".

• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid solution on lab cluster (PR #205, merged 2026-06-23): The new TelcoBoundaryClock test scenario (introduced on main only) requires at least one node with a WPC NIC (Intel E810/E830) satisfying a 5-step constraint chain. Nodes cnfdf30/31/32 do not satisfy these constraints, so BeforeAll fails with no T-BC solution found for TBC after all three retries, aborting both the tbc serial suite and the all parallel suite's SynchronizedBeforeSuite.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualnicbctbcall
BC FREERUN cascade (clock class 248)Yes (dominant)NoNo
pmc CLI invocation bug (3b93c9f)Yes (3 tests)NoNo
T-BC hardware topology miss (PR #205)NoYes (suite abort)Yes (BeforeAll abort)

• The BC FREERUN and pmc bug failures are exclusive to dualnicbc — they require a dual-NIC BC hardware topology that the other suites do not exercise.
• The T-BC solver failure is shared identically between tbc and all: both abort at setup with the same error string from testconfig.go:1899, caused by the same missing WPC-NIC hardware on the same nodes (cnfdf30/31/32).
• All three root causes originate from main-branch changes (test scripts run from TEST_BRANCH=main against release-5.0 images). No production image regression is implicated.


**

Actionable Recommendations:**


1. Fix the pmc invocation immediately (commit 3b93c9f): In test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go, change the ExecCommand call to pass the management command as a single string argument — []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} — or pipe it via stdin. This unblocks 3 deterministic failures with a one-line change and restores the clock-ID fallback path.

2. Add a hardware-availability guard to the tbc/all BeforeAll: Replace the Fail() call when solutions["TBC"] is empty with a Skip() so that absence of WPC-NIC hardware causes the suite to be skipped rather than failing the entire describe block. This converts 2 hard failures to expected skips without requiring cluster reprovisioning. (PR #205 introduced Fail; the fix should mirror how other suites handle missing hardware modes.)

3. Investigate the BC FREERUN root cause (dominant, ~33 failures): Determine whether the BC's ptp4l clock-class 248 / ens2f0np0-as-MASTER state is caused by a lab infrastructure regression (e.g., grandmaster unreachable, wrong VLAN, upstream link flap) or a software change in linuxptp-daemon or `cloud---

TL;DR: Two main-branch test regressions (PMC invocation bug, T-BC hardware mismatch) plus a BC FREERUN cascade account for all 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~26/38 failures — BC ptp4l stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), ens2f0np0 not recovering to SLAVE: The boundary clock on the dualnicbc suite never acquires lock, cascading into sync, event, metric, and recovery test failures across the suite. This is the dominant failure mode and is likely an infra/hardware or config regression in the test cluster.

• ~8/38 failures — PMC command invocation bug introduced by commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC() in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go passes GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate CLI positional arguments; pmc rejects both as bad command. Every call to this fallback deterministically fails, causing Eventually timeouts in sync tests. Affects at minimum three tests in dualnicbc and any other suite invoking the same helper.

• ~4/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no WPC-NIC solution on lab cluster (cnfdf30–32): Introduced by PR #205 (merged 2026-06-23, main only). The SynchronizedBeforeSuite / BeforeAll aborts with no T-BC solution found for TBC in both the tbc and all suites because no node satisfies the five-step WPC-NIC + multi-port + same-LAN constraints. Confirmed non-flake — retries all fail identically.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualnicbctbcall
BC FREERUN / no SLAVE lock✅ (dominant)——
PMC invocation bug (3b93c9f)✅possiblypossibly
T-BC topology / no WPC NIC—✅✅

• The T-BC topology failure is shared across tbc and all — the same PtpConfigTelcoBC() code path and the same hardware gap produce identical errors in both suites.
• The BC FREERUN and PMC bug are dualnicbc-specific in the evidence available, though the PMC helper is shared code and could surface in other suites if the log-stream fallback is triggered there too.
• No failures appear to be genuine flakes — all three root causes are deterministic given current cluster state and current main test code.


**




Per-Suite Details



dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Three tests fail because of a broken pmc invocation introduced by a main-branch test script commit; the remaining eight failures stem from the BC's ptp4l being stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248) with ens2f0np0 reporting MASTER instead of SLAVE, cascading into event, metric, and recovery test failures.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PMC command invocation bug in test helper (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET


grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: Commit 3b93c9f ("tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") introduced getClockIDViaPMC() in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go (line 143) as a fallback when log-stream parsing fails. It invokes pmc via pods.ExecCommand with []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} — passing GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as two separate positional arguments. The pmc binary does not accept management commands as CLI arguments; it reads them interactively. Both tokens are rejected as bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET, so re.FindStringSubmatch never matches and the function always returns an error. This causes the outer Eventually in the sync tests to timeout waiting for a clock ID that can never be read. The bug is only on main (confirmed: no release-5.0 remote branch exists in the ptp-operator clone; check_backport.py exited non-zero). Since the test scripts run from TEST_BRANCH=main, this directly breaks the CI job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: 3b93c9f (nocturnalastro, Jun 9 2026) — test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go. GitHub: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/3b93c9f408647a8b55d37920103c57a3e2cb11c2
• Is it a flake? No — the bug is deterministic; every call to the PMC fallback will fail.
• Suggested Fix: Change the invocation to pass the management command as a single string argument: []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} — or use stdin piping — so pmc receives it as an interactive command rather than individual CLI tokens.

• Group 2: BC ptp4l stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248); ens2f0np0 not recovering to SLAVE (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "Should recover clockClass via event API after cloud-event-proxy crash", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage", "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)


Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM

Expected : 0 to equal : 1


Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

```

Clock class 248 is ptp4l's FREERUN/uncalibrated value. ens2f0np0 is the BC's upstream (slave) port toward the GM; it reporting MASTER role means ptp4l elected itself as best master — indicating no GM announce frames are being received. phc2sys status 0 follows directly: it does not run when ptp4l is in FREERUN. PTP metrics and event API tests also fail because no lock-state changes are published. The same pattern (ens2f0np0 MASTER, clock class 248) appears identically in the dualnicbcha suite, confirming this is not suite-level state pollution but a node-level PTP convergence failure.

• Root Cause: After the outage recovery test takes ens2f0np0 down and back up, ptp4l on the BC node (cnfdf32) does not re-slave to the grandmaster within the 300 s timeout and remains in MASTER role (clock class 248). All subsequent tests in the suite run against an unlocked BC: clock class checks see 248, phc2sys is absent (status 0), events are not published, and the cloud-event-proxy recovery test also finds clock class ≠ 6. This is cross-suite (dualnicbc + dualnicbcha) consistent, pointing to either a ptp4l / linuxptp-daemon release-5.0 regression in port role reconciliation after link-flap, or a test-lab hardware/network issue on cnfdf32 where the GM link does not reliably recover.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the release-5.0 production images are opaque here (only the upstream main linuxptp-daemon clone is available locally, not the downstream release-5.0 fork). The most recent linuxptp-daemon merge on main is fd6ff1e (CNF-22056, CNF-22693, OCPBUGS-85586, OCPBUGS-93744 — upstream sync 2026-06-30). Whether this synced into release-5.0 images requires checking the downstream repo.
• Is it a flake? Possibly — outage-recovery tests are sensitive to link-recovery timing and lab hardware state. However, consistent cross-suite failure (dualnicbc + dualnicbcha) on the same run without recovery makes an environment flake less likely than a real regression.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate ptp4l port state machine behavior after ip link set down/up on the BC upstream port in release-5.0 images; add an explicit BMCA wait after link-up in the test outage-recovery helper (ptptesthelper.go) to allow port election to complete before asserting SLAVE role.

• Group 3: Event consumer pod log read timeout (affects: "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "[dualnicbc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync")
• Evidence:

```

error reading logs in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer: context deadline exceeded


could not push initial event, err=error reading logs in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: Both event-based tests rely on the consumer pod (in ptp-event-framework-test namespace) emitting a SyncStateChange log line after linuxptp-daemon restarts or publishes an initial event. Since the BC is in FREERUN (Group 2), ptp4l never transitions to LOCKED state, so cloud-event-proxy never publishes a lock-state event, and the consumer pod produces no matching output. The log-stream reader times out with context deadline exceeded. This is a cascade failure downstream of Group 2, not an independent bug.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Downstream of Group 2.
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic cascade from the BC not locking.
• Suggested Fix: Fix the BC lock issue (Group 2); the consumer timeout will resolve once PTP sync events are actually published.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression (Group 1 — deterministic test script bug) + test_failure (Groups 2 & 3 — PTP convergence failure, possibly production regression)


Suite Severity: HIGH — 11/11 dualnicbc tests affected across two distinct root causes; Group 1 is an immediate test-script regression requiring a code fix on main; Group 2 blocks all clock-sync-dependent tests and is shared with the dualnicbcha suite.


tbc



Suite: tbc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The sole TBC test failure is a hard setup error — the L2 topology discovery finds zero valid T-BC topologies in the lab cluster, so the BeforeAll block aborts before the reboot test can run.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: T-BC topology not discoverable in CI lab hardware (affects: [It] [tbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Reboot discovery The slave node is rebooted and discovered and in sync [Serial])
• Evidence:

```

junit-failures.txt

Test: [SynchronizedBeforeSuite]

Message: Could not create a ptp config

Expected

<*errors.errorString | 0xc00090dc00>:

no T-BC solution found for TBC


Test: [It] [tbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Reboot discovery

The slave node is rebooted and discovered and in sync [Serial]

Message: Could not create a ptp config, err=no T-BC solution found for TBC

```

• Root Cause: CreatePtpConfigurationsWithRetry(3) is called in ptp.go:364 inside a BeforeAll; it delegates to PtpConfigTelcoBC() in testconfig.go:1891-1899. That function immediately returns fmt.Errorf("no T-BC solution found for %s", BestSolution) when data.solutions["TBC"] is empty (line 1898-1899). The solutions map is populated by the constraint-solver at suite startup — it requires at least one node with a WPC NIC that has a PTP receiver port, two transmitter ports on the same physical NIC, and a grandmaster reachable on the same L2 LAN (all enforced by StepIsWPCNic + StepSameNic + StepSameLan2 constraints in testconfig.go:704-710). The worker nodes in this run (cnfdf30/31/32) do not satisfy those hardware constraints, so the solver returns an empty solution set and the entire PTP e2e tests describe-block is aborted via Fail().
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/205 (aede1e9 — "Merge pull request #205 from nocturnalastro/tbc_ci_tests", merged 2026-06-23 on main). This PR introduced the TelcoBoundaryClock test mode, PtpConfigTelcoBC(), and the associated solver constraints. The test scripts run from main so PR #205 is active; however, it was not required to be cherry-picked to release-5.0 since only test code (not production images) is sourced from main. The check_backport.py script was not found in the workspace so automated cherry-pick verification could not be executed.
• Is it a flake? No. The error is deterministic: three retries (`CreatePtp
2026-07-13T00:01
❓ infra
❌ failure5.05.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream248mtest_regression CRITICAL
Grandmaster node cnfdf32 failed to LOCK (clock class 248), cascading to all dualfollower sync failures; TBC suite aborted due to missing WPC NIC topology in the CI cluster.
38 failed tests
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)

linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```

In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```

TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)

linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia


Based on the suite analyses in the prompt, here is the cross-suite summary:



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.



Cross-Suite Patterns


Root causedualfollowertbcallNotes
Interface alias mismatch (fd6ff1e)Yes (4 explicit)LikelyLikelyPervasive — any test querying openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw iface label fails
T-BC topology absentNoYes (1)Yes (1)Suite-specific to TBC tests; SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort means no TBC test can pass regardless

The interface alias regression is cross-suite because openshift_ptp_clock_state metric lookups by raw interface name are used throughout ptp-operator conformance tests. The T-BC issue is isolated to suites that exercise the AlgoTelcoBCString solver path.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.


3. Gate the T-BC test job on lab hardware capabilities.

Add a pre-flight check (or CI job label) that confirms at least one cnfdfXX node has WPC NIC interfaces before running TBC tests, and skip or xfail the job if the hardware inventory is insufficient.



Overall Issue Class

test_regression


Overall Severity

HIGH — Two deterministic regressions introduced within one week of each other account for all 38 failures; no flakes involved. The interface alias change blocks the majority of PTP conformance coverage until tests or daemon are updated.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because a June 30 upstream sync PR introduced interface aliasing in linuxptp-daemon that changes openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels from raw interface names (e.g., ens2f0np0) to NIC-level aliases (e.g., ens2fx), while the test code still queries raw names.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics: ...

```

And from the sync PR commit stats:

```

pkg/alias/alias.go | 47 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/alias/manager.go | 141 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/utils/utils.go | 51 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/daemon/metrics.go | 839 + (NEW FILE)

```

(All introduced by commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30, 2026.)

• Root Cause: PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, "upstream-sync-2026-06-30") introduced a new interface alias system (pkg/alias/manager.go, pkg/utils/utils.go). GetAliasValue("ens2f0np0") → "ens2fx" and GetAliasValue("ens2f3np3") → "ens2fx" (both dual-follower slave ports on the same NIC resolve to the same alias). masterOffsetInterface.set() in pkg/daemon/metrics.go:801-808 now stores alias.GetAlias(ifname) instead of the raw name, so updateClockStateMetrics() (line 527-538) emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2fx"} instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}. The conformance tests in openshift/ptp-operator@main still look up metrics by raw interface name, finding nothing.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (PR #617, merged 2026-06-30). This is on the upstream main branch that the CI job builds directly (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git); the release-5.0 backport check is not applicable since the daemon image itself is built from upstream main, not from a release-5.0 branch.
• Is it a flake? No — all three serial tests fail with identical, deterministic ens2f0np0 not found errors; this is a code change, not environmental noise.
• Suggested Fix: Update the ptp-operator conformance test helper that looks up openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics to resolve the slave interface name through GetAliasValue() (or the same alias lookup the daemon uses) before querying Prometheus, so tests use ens2fx instead of ens2f0np0. Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Group 2: OS Clock never reached LOCKED (event-based parallel test) (affects: "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [dualfollower-parallel])
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

• Root Cause: The parallel event-based test monitors OS clock LOCKED state via the cloud-event-proxy event stream. Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Same as Group 1 — fd6ff1e in k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.
• Is it a flake? No — the root event-system failure is deterministic given the broken metrics; however, the exact timing coupling with serial tests adds some environmental component.
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)
